entities.name: TCGA molecular classification
synonyms: The Cancer Genome Atlas molecular classification

entities.name: https://ror.org/00nyxxr91grid.412474.00000 0001 0027 0586Key Laboratory of Carcinogenesis and Translational Research (Ministry of Education), Department of Gynecologic Oncology, Peking University Cancer Hospital & Institute, Beijing, China
Integrating homologous recombination deficiency subtyping with TCGA molecular classification for enhanced prognostic stratification and personalised therapy in endometrial cancer

evidence_entities.text: HRD subtyping identified distinct genomic, pathological, and immunological features. HRD-High tumours were associated with advanced FIGO stages, TP53 mutations, higher chromosomal instability, and elevated CD8⁺PD-1⁺ T-cell infiltration.
HRD subtyping independently predicted disease-free survival and showed superior prognostic accuracy (C-index = 0.857) compared to TCGA subtyping (C-index = 0.751).
Compared to the other two groups, the HRD-high group exhibited higher mutation frequencies in TP53, RAD51, and SPOP, but fewer mutations in PTEN, ARID1A, PIK3R1, CTNNB1, and CTCF
The HRD score was strongly positively correlated with genomic instability markers, such as SCIN (R = 0.93, p < 0.01) and aneuploidy score (R = 0.83, p < 0.001).

notes: This study focuses on integrating HRD subtyping with TCGA molecular classification in endometrial cancer
The research was conducted at Peking University Cancer Hospital with 142 patients
HRD scoring was based on genomic scar metrics: LOH, TAI, and LST
The study demonstrates superior prognostic accuracy of HRD subtyping compared to TCGA classification alone
